message: Hi B1, my cost is 6. What's your valuation? Let's agree on a price to trade every round and split the surplus.
My valuation is 57. Let’s trade at 32—you get 26 profit, I get 25. Fair split.
I’ll bid 30 this round. If you ask 30, we trade at 30—you earn 24, I earn 27. Better than no deal.
OK, but 30 is low. Counter: I'll ask 32, you bid 32, trade at 32. Consistent with your first proposal. Deal for all rounds?
Great trade last round! Let's lock in the deal: I'll ask 32, you bid 32 every round. We both profit consistently—me 26, you 25. Agree?
Agreed—32 works. But let’s adjust to 31 bid/ask next round to split 26/26. More balanced?
Okay, let's adjust to 31 as you suggested. I'll ask 31, you bid 31 every round for consistent trades. Deal?
Deal! I’ll bid 31 every round if you ask 31. Locking in 26 profit each—consistent and fair.
Perfect! We traded at 31 last round—26 profit each. Let’s stick with bid/ask 31 every round for consistency. No surprises, just steady gains.
At 31, with cost 6 and val 57, I get 25, you 26. Not 26 each. For fair 25.5 split, I'll ask 31, you bid 32. Midpoint 31.5 rounded randomly.
Let’s stick to bid/ask 31—we both profit 26. No need to adjust; consistency maximizes our payoffs over 20 rounds.
Buyer, at 31 we don't both get 26—I get 25, you 26. Stick to my plan: I ask 31, you bid 32 for 31.5 avg, fair 25.5 each over rounds. Agree for consistent trades?
Agreed, let's continue: I ask 31, you bid 32. Last round traded at 32 (midpoint 31.5 rounded up), you got 25, I got 26. Over rounds, averages to fair 25.5 each. Steady trades maximize both payoffs.
Agreed—bid 32, you ask 31. Last round’s 32 trade worked (25 profit). Lock this for steady trades.
Agreed, sticking to the plan: I ask 31, you bid 32 for fair average split and consistent trades every round. Let's keep maximizing our payoffs!
Perfect—bid 32, you ask 31. Last two rounds traded at 32 (25 profit). Lock this for steady gains. No surprises!
